title: Fix dummy environment agent team features — automatic decomposition

stories.title: Dummy provider Chat() returns valid decomposition JSON
stories.description: When the Decomposer calls provider.Chat() with the decomposition system prompt, the dummy provider must return a valid JSON task array instead of markdown demo text.
acceptanceCriteria: dummy.Provider.Chat() detects decomposition requests (system prompt contains 'task decomposition engine')
Returns a valid JSON array of tasks with id, description, category, blocked_by, priority fields
The returned tasks include realistic dependencies (exploration → implementation → verification)
Existing non-decomposition Chat() behavior (agent loop) is unchanged
go build succeeds with no errors

stories.title: End-to-end decompose→run integration test with dummy provider
stories.description: Add a test that calls OrchestratorService.Decompose() backed by the dummy provider and verifies tasks are created, then runs the DAG to completion.
acceptanceCriteria: Test calls Decompose(ctx, goal) and verifies it returns >0 tasks without error
Test verifies the decomposed tasks are in the graph with assigned agent types
Test runs the full DAG via Run() and verifies all tasks reach terminal status

stories.title: Dummy provider Chat() response includes goal-aware task descriptions
stories.description: The dummy decomposition response should incorporate the user's goal text into task descriptions for realistic demo behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Decomposition response references the user's goal in at least one task description
Task IDs are valid kebab-case identifiers
Categories are valid TaskCategory values (explore, implement, verify, etc.)
Blocked_by references are internally consistent (no dangling refs)
go test ./... -short passes with all existing tests still green
